description_long: This object is a decorative pennant banner consisting of a series of small, triangular flags strung along a thin cord. The pennants are evenly spaced and appear to be made of a lightweight fabric or plastic, each flag featuring a deep blue color. The cord is attached at both ends to small, block-like mounts, which are likely designed to be affixed to a wall or post. The flags hang downward in a gentle arc, suggesting flexibility in the cord and a lightweight construction. The mounts at each end are solid and provide structural support for the entire length of the banner. This type of pennant is commonly used for festive decoration in urban environments such as parks, streets, or event spaces.
description: A string of blue triangular pennant flags suspended on a cord, attached at both ends to small mounts. The banner is lightweight and designed for decorative use in urban settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full arc of blue triangular pennants strung between two mounts.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thinness of the cord and the block-like shape of the end mounts.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually similar to the front, displaying the pennant flags and their spacing along the cord.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again highlighting the slim profile of the cord and the mounts at each end.
attribute_graphic_design: The pennant features a repeating pattern of solid blue triangular flags with no visible text or symbols. The flags are evenly spaced along a dark cord.
